title: Day 05 Quiz — React Router and Nested Layouts
description: Verify you understand SPA routing, Link, Outlet, nested routes, and navigation before Day 06 custom hooks.

questions.prompt: What is a Single Page Application (SPA) in React?

options.text: An app with only one component
An app that loads once and updates views client-side without full page reloads
An app that cannot use APIs

questions.explanation: SPAs swap views via client-side routing. The server typically serves index.html once; React Router changes the URL and rendered route.

questions.prompt: React Router's Link component causes a full browser page reload like a native <a href> to another origin.
questions.explanation: Link intercepts navigation and updates history client-side without reloading the document — core SPA behavior.

questions.prompt: What does Outlet render in React Router v6?

options.text: The matched child route element in a nested layout

questions.explanation: Outlet is a placeholder where child routes render inside a parent layout — sidebar stays, content area swaps.

questions.prompt: Which belong in a dashboard nested route layout? Select all that apply.

options.text: Full page reload on every link click

questions.explanation: Layout route wraps persistent chrome; Outlet renders changing page content. SPA avoids full reloads.

questions.prompt: Use {{blank1}} instead of <a> for internal app navigation, and {{blank2}} for unknown paths.
questions.explanation: Link preserves SPA navigation; a catch-all route like path='*' renders 404.

questions.prompt: Complete the layout component import for nested child rendering.
questions.explanation: Outlet renders matched child routes inside a parent layout route.

questions.prompt: NavLink is preferred over Link when you need:

options.text: Active styling based on current route

questions.explanation: NavLink applies active className or style when its to prop matches the current location.

questions.prompt: Why use nested routes instead of conditionally rendering pages in App.tsx?
questions.sampleAnswer: Nested routes sync UI with the URL, enable back/forward and deep links, keep layout DRY with Outlet, and scale as routes grow without giant switch statements.
questions.explanation: Interviewers want URL-driven architecture, not manual page state.

questions.prompt: Clicking sidebar Link goes to /profile but URL changes and content stays on Dashboard. Likely cause?

options.text: Route path typo, wrong Outlet placement, or child route not registered

questions.explanation: URL changes mean router works; content stuck means route config or Outlet mismatch — classic Day 05 bug.

questions.prompt: What does closure enable in an event handler inside a route component?
questions.explanation: handleClick closes over title from the render where it was created — fundamental JS for handlers in routed components.
